title: Cape Verde at the 2026 World Cup · lineup & formation | MyLineups
original_text:
Drawn into Group H, Cape Verde head into the 2026 World Cup in a 4-1-4-1, coached by Bubista.

It is a squad drawn from 26 clubs worldwide, averaging 31.3 years, and worth roughly 9.1 million combined. Plot the XI below, then open it in the free builder to shape your own Cape Verde lineup for the tournament.

Cape Verde line up for the FIFA World Cup 2026 in Group H in a 4-1-4-1 , with a back 4 and Vozinha in goal.

In defence: Borges, Lopes, Moreira, Costa, Pina, Pires, and Stopira.

In midfield: Lopes Cabral, Monteiro, Pina, Duarte, Duarte, Paulo, and Semedo.

In attack: Arcanjo, Benchimol, Mendes, Rodrigues, Cabral, da Costa, Livramento, Semedo, and Varela.

The 26-man squad pulls players from 26 clubs across world football. The starting XI averages 31.3 years and is worth roughly 9.1 million combined.

Bubista (CPV) is the coach guiding this side.

Cape Verde stays tied to the World Cup hub and opens directly in the builder if you want to reshape the tournament XI — free on the web or in the mobile app.

Open Cape Verde in the free MyLineups builder, reshape the 4-1-4-1, pick your captain and export a clean image.
